title: Kali monopterum
intro: Kali monopterum, also known as the Kalanum or Kalanum latifolium, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Menispermaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Kali monopterum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Kali monopterum is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is commonly used to make herbal remedies such as tonics, ointments, and infusions.
taxonomy: Kali monopterum is a species of plant within the family Amaranthaceae. It is a type of succulent that belongs to the genus Kali. The classification of Kali monopterum is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and then into the phylum Magnoliophyta. This plant is native to tropical regions, specifically in India and parts of Africa. Kali monopterum's classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Kali monopterum, also known as the Devil's Backbone, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. The plant is said to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its leaves and roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. parts: Kali monopterum, also known as the silver oak or white jade tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used medicinally.
